<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Presidents of major operating subsidiaries, including Messrs.&nbsp;Staffieri</P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:1pt; margin-left:2.00em; text-indent:-1.00em; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">and&nbsp;DeCampli</P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-size:8pt">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" ALIGN="center">2x</TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Executive officers at a particular guideline level must attain their minimum Equity Guidelines level by the end of their fifth
anniversary at that level. If an executive does not attain the guideline level within the applicable period, he or she must not sell any shares of PPL common stock and will be required to retain shares acquired upon the exercise of stock options or
upon the lapsing of restrictions on shares of restricted stock, restricted stock units or performance units, in each case net of required tax withholding, until the guideline level is achieved. In addition, annual cash incentives awarded after that
date may be made in restricted stock unit grants until actual ownership meets or exceeds the guideline level. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The Equity Guidelines encourage increased stock
ownership on the part of the executive officers, which further aligns the interests of management and shareowners. All named executive officers were in compliance with the Equity Guidelines as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The company also has a policy that prohibits its officers and directors from pledging shares of company stock as collateral for any loans, from engaging in any form of
hedging transaction or otherwise trading in derivatives of PPL common stock. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><I><A NAME="toc651955_51"></A>Recoupment Policy </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The Committee adopted a policy in January 2013 regarding recoupment of executive compensation. Subject to the discretion and approval of the Board, the company will seek
recoupment of any incentive-based compensation awarded to any current executive officer of the company where the Board has determined that: </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="5%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">the company is required to prepare an accounting restatement due to the material noncompliance by the company with any financial reporting requirement under the securities laws; and </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">a lower award would have been made to the executive officer based upon the restated financial results. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The Board has
full and final authority to make all determinations under this policy, including, without limitation, whether the policy applies and, if so, the amount of cash bonus or other incentive-based compensation, if any, to be repaid by any executive
officer. In each such instance as determined by the Board, the company will, to the extent permitted by applicable law, seek to recover incentive-based </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL" ALIGN="right">PPL Corporation 2014 Proxy

remedies that may be available to the company, including such remedies contained in the company&#146;s equity grant and employment letters, if any, and applicable law. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><I><A NAME="toc651955_52"></A>Other Compensation Matters </I></B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><I><A NAME="toc651955_53">
</A>Perquisites and Other Benefits </I></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">In addition to its direct compensation program,&nbsp;which includes salary, annual cash incentive and long-term incentive
opportunities, the company provides indirect executive benefits such as non-qualified deferred compensation opportunities and supplemental executive retirement plan benefits for executives. The PPL Corporation Supplemental Executive Retirement Plan,
or SERP, was closed to new officers after December&nbsp;31, 2011. Additionally, PPL provides carefully selected executive perquisites, consistent with market practices, which serve a direct business interest, such as financial planning services to
assist executives, who generally have more complex financial situations than most employees, and severance protection in the event of termination of employment under limited circumstances. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Officers of the company, including the named executive officers, are eligible for company-paid financial planning services. These services include financial planning,
tax preparation support and, for each named executive officer, a one-time payment for estate documentation preparation. These services are provided in recognition of time constraints on busy executives and their more complex compensation program
that requires professional financial and tax planning. We believe that good financial planning by experts reduces the amount of time and attention that executive officers must spend on such issues. Such planning also helps ensure that the objectives
of our compensation programs are met and not hindered by unexpected tax or other consequences. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">All of the named executive officers are also eligible for an
executive physical every two years, not to exceed a cost of $5,000. The Committee believes that the benefit is beneficial to both the employee and to the company through reduced costs. Executive physical examinations offer a more thorough and
intensive health&nbsp;screening, are comprehensive and include tests for rare&nbsp;diseases. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2013, Mr.&nbsp;Staffieri received a one-time, lump-sum payment of
$50,000 in lieu of specified LKE executive benefits that he will forego going forward. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The incremental cost to us of all perquisites received by our named
executive officers for 2013 is summarized in Note&nbsp;7 to the Summary Compensation Table. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><I><A NAME="toc651955_54"></A>Indirect Compensation </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Officers of the company, including the named executive officers, participate in benefit programs offered to all PPL employees, or in the case of Mr.&nbsp;Staffieri, all
LKE employees. In addition, officers are eligible for the executive benefit plans discussed below. LKE executive benefit plans are identified and discussed separately below. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">For the named executive officers other than Mr.&nbsp;Staffieri, the primary retirement income program consists of two plans: (1)&nbsp;the PPL Retirement Plan, a
tax-qualified, defined benefit pension plan available to employees of the company generally; and (2)&nbsp;the Supplemental Executive Retirement Plan, or SERP, a nonqualified defined benefit pension plan available for officers of the company.
For&nbsp;Mr.&nbsp;Farr, who is vested in the PPL Retirement Plan, but not vested in the SERP, the Supplemental Compensation Pension Plan, which is a non-qualified plan that is available to employees of the
</P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">46&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;PPL Corporation 2014
Proxy Statement </P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">
company generally, is also available to him until he becomes vested in the SERP. In 2011, the company made a change for salaried new hires, including executive officers, by closing the PPL
Retirement Plan, the Supplemental Compensation Pension Plan and the SERP to new entrants and adopting the new PPL Retirement Savings Plan. This change, however, did not affect any of the named executive officers as they were serving as executive
officers prior to 2011. Additional details on these plans are provided under &#147;Executive Compensation Tables&nbsp;&#151; Pension Benefits in 2013&#148; at page&nbsp;60. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The primary capital accumulation opportunities for executive officers are: (1)&nbsp;stock gains under the company&#146;s long-term incentive program and employee stock
ownership plan; and (2)&nbsp;voluntary savings opportunities that, for 2013, included savings through the tax-qualified employee savings plan, which is a 401(k) plan (our PPL Deferred Savings Plan), and the Executive Deferred Compensation Plan,
which is a nonqualified deferred compensation arrangement. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Under the PPL Deferred Savings Plan, the company provides matching cash contributions of up to 3% of the
participating employee&#146;s pay (defined as salary plus annual cash incentive award) subject to contribution limits imposed by federal tax rules. Participating employees are vested in the company&#146;s matching contributions after one year of
service. This plan provides a selection of core investment options, including publicly available mutual funds, institutionally managed funds and &#147;lifestyle funds&#148; available from a mutual fund provider. The plan investment options also
include a brokerage account option that allows participants to select from a broad range of publicly available mutual funds, including those of the plan trustee as well as competitor funds. Participants may request distribution of their accounts at
any time following termination of employment. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Our Executive Deferred Compensation Plan permits participants to defer a portion or all of their cash compensation in
excess of the estimated minimum legally required annual payroll tax withholding. A hypothetical account is established for each participant who elects to defer, and the participant selects one or more investment choices that generally mirror those
that are available generally to employees under the PPL Deferred Savings Plan. For additional details on the Executive Deferred Compensation Plan, see the &#147;Executive Compensation Tables&nbsp;&#151; Nonqualified Deferred Compensation in
2013&#148; table on page&nbsp;67. Matching contributions are made under this plan on behalf of participating officers to make up for matching contributions that would have been made on behalf of such officers under the PPL Deferred Savings Plan but
for the imposition of maximum statutory limits on qualified plan benefits (for example, annual limits on eligible pay and contributions). Executive officers who reach the maximum limits in the PPL Deferred Savings Plan are generally eligible for
matching contributions under the Executive Deferred Compensation Plan. There is no vesting requirement for the company matching contributions. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The company also has
a tax-qualified employee stock ownership plan, the PPL Employee Stock Ownership Plan, or ESOP, to which the company may make an annual contribution. Historically, the company has contributed a dollar amount to the ESOP that is equal to the tax
benefit it receives for a tax deduction on dividends paid on PPL common stock held by the trustee of the ESOP. Contributions are then allocated among the ESOP participants based on the following two measures: (1)&nbsp;the amount of total dividends
paid on the participant&#146;s account; and (2)&nbsp;a pro rata amount based on salary up to a median salary amount. The total allocation cannot exceed 5% of a participant&#146;s compensation. The ESOP trustee invests exclusively in the
company&#146;s common stock. All named executive officers except Mr.&nbsp;Staffieri participated in the ESOP during 2013. Shares held for a minimum of 36&nbsp;months are available for withdrawal, and participants may request distribution of their
account at any time following termination of employment. There is no vesting period for contributions made under the ESOP. The participant has the option of receiving the actual shares of common stock or the cash equivalent of such shares at the
time of withdrawal or distribution. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL" ALIGN="right">PPL Corporation 2014 Proxy
Statement &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;47 </P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">There are similar employee benefits, such as a retirement plan, a savings plan, a supplemental executive retirement plan
and a non-qualified deferred compensation plan, in place for Mr.&nbsp;Staffieri and other LKE executives that provide retirement income benefits and capital accumulation opportunities for executives. See &#147;Executive Compensation Tables &#151;
Pension Benefits in 2013 &#151; LG&amp;E and KU Retirement Plan&#148; at page&nbsp;62 and &#147;&#151;&nbsp;LG&amp;E and KU Supplemental Executive Retirement Plan&#148; on page&nbsp;63, and the description of the LG&amp;E and KU Nonqualified Savings
Plan at &#147;Executive Compensation Tables &#151; Nonqualified Deferred Compensation in 2013&#148; at page&nbsp;66. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><I><A NAME="toc651955_55"></A>Special
Compensation </I></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">In addition to the annual direct and indirect compensation described above, the company provides special compensation under specific situations.
</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><U>Hiring and Retention.</U> As part of the executive recruiting process, the company may make offers of employment to new executive candidates that will attract
talent to the company and compensate these candidates for compensation they may lose when terminating employment with their prior employer. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Generally, annual
compensation for new executive officers is consistent with that of current executives in similar positions. Incentive awards for the year of hire are generally prorated for the period of service during the executive&#146;s initial year of employment
and paid out after the end of the year. One-time awards may be made in restricted stock or restricted stock units to replace awards a new executive may be losing from a former employer or as part of a sign-on award to encourage an executive to join
the company. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">In limited circumstances, generally involving mid-career hiring or as part of a merger or acquisition, the company may enter into retention agreements
with key executives to encourage their long-term employment with the company. The intention is to retain key executives for the long term and focus the executive&#146;s attention on stock price growth during the retention period. These agreements
typically involve the grant of restricted stock or restricted stock units on which the restrictions lapse after a period of time that may vary on a case-by-case basis. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Individual awards vary based on an executive&#146;s level, company service and the need for retention, as well as the market demand for an executive&#146;s talent. The
amount of an award is typically a multiple of salary converted to a special equity grant based on the closing price of PPL common stock on the NYSE as of the grant date. For specific details on retention agreements that are outstanding for named
executive officers, see &#147;Retention Agreements&#148; on page&nbsp;71. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><U>Employment Agreements.</U> We generally do not enter into traditional employment
agreements with executive officers. There are no specific agreements pertaining to length of employment that would commit the company to pay an executive for a specific period. Generally, our executive officers are &#147;employees-at-will&#148;
whose employment is conditioned on performance and subject to termination by the company at any time. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><U>Change in Control Protections.</U> The company believes
executive officers who are terminated without cause or who resign for &#147;good reason&#148; (as defined in &#147;Change in Control Arrangements&#148; below at page&nbsp;68) in connection with a change in control of PPL Corporation should be
provided separation benefits. These benefits are intended to ensure that executives focus on serving the company and shareowner interests without the distraction of possible job and income loss. All of our named executive officers have separation
benefits in the event of a change in control. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">48&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;PPL Corporation 2014
Proxy Statement </P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The major components of the company&#146;s change in control protections are: </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="5%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">accelerated vesting of specific outstanding equity awards in order to protect executives&#146; equity-based award value from an acquirer; </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="5%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">severance benefits;&nbsp;and </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="5%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">trusts to fund promised obligations in order to protect executive compensation from an acquirer. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The 2012 Stock
Incentive Plan eliminated the acceleration of the vesting of outstanding equity awards granted under the plan upon a change in control, unless there is a qualifying termination of employment. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The company&#146;s change in control benefits are consistent with the practices of companies with whom PPL competes for talent and assist in retaining executives and
recruiting new executives to the company. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><I><U>Accelerated Vesting of Equity Awards</U>.</I> As of the close of a transaction that results in a change in control
of PPL Corporation, all outstanding equity awards granted under the former Incentive Compensation Plan, or ICP, as part of the company&#146;s compensation program (excluding restricted stock and restricted stock units issued pursuant to retention
agreements) become available to executives. As a result, the vesting and exercisability of stock awards and option awards granted as part of the long-term incentive program accelerate&nbsp;&#151; in other words, restrictions on all outstanding
restricted stock units lapse, a pro rata portion of performance units become payable and all unexercisable stock options become exercisable. Under the 2012 Stock Incentive Plan, equity awards granted under this plan do not automatically become
available to executives as of a change in control, unless the executive is terminated (a &#147;double trigger&#148;). Stock options granted prior to 2007 are exercisable for 36&nbsp;months following a qualifying termination of employment in
connection with a change in control; options granted in 2007 and thereafter under the ICP are, after a change in control, exercisable for the remaining term of the stock option. Stock options granted under the 2012 Stock Incentive Plan are
exercisable for the remaining term of the stock option or five years after retirement, whichever is earlier. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><I><U>Severance Benefits</U>.</I> To continue to
retain and protect our executives, the company adopted an Executive Severance Plan in 2012 that provides severance benefits for officers, including the named executive officers, terminated for reasons other than cause. The key features of the plan
that apply to all of the named executive officers include (1)&nbsp;two years of base pay; (2)&nbsp;an allowance for benefit continuation; and (3)&nbsp;outplacement or career services support. Severance benefits payable under this program are
conditioned on the executive officer agreeing to release the company from any liability arising from the employment relationship. Additional details on current arrangements for named executive officers are discussed under &#147;Termination
Benefits&#148; below at page&nbsp;72. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The company has change in control agreements with all of the named executive officers that provide benefits to the executives
upon specified terminations of employment in connection with a change in control of PPL Corporation. The benefits provided under these agreements replace any other severance benefits provided to these officers by PPL Corporation, including the
Executive Severance Plan or any prior severance agreement. Additional details on the terms of these agreements are described in &#147;Change in Control Arrangements&#148; at page&nbsp;68. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><I><U>Rabbi Trust</U>. </I>The company has entered into trust arrangements that currently cover some PPL compensation programs, namely the SERP, the Executive Deferred
Compensation Plan, the change in control agreements and the Directors Deferred Compensation Plan. These trust arrangements provide </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL" ALIGN="right">PPL Corporation 2014 Proxy
Statement &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;49 </P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">
that specified trusts are to be funded when a &#147;change in control&#148; occurs. See &#147;Change in Control Arrangements&#148; at page&nbsp;68 for a description of change in control events.
</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The trusts, which specifically relate to programs managed by PPL and do not currently cover the LKE programs, are currently unfunded but would become funded upon
the occurrence of a change in control. The trust arrangements provide for immediate funding of benefits upon the occurrence of a potential change in control, and further provide that the trusts, which are funded upon a potential change in control,
can be revoked and the contributions returned if a change in control in fact does not occur. There are no current plans to fund any of the trusts. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><I><A NAME="toc651955_56">
</A>Tax and Accounting Considerations </I></B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><I>Section&nbsp;162(m). </I>Section&nbsp;162(m) of the Internal Revenue Code generally provides that publicly held
corporations may not deduct in any taxable year specified compensation in excess of $1,000,000 paid to the principal executive officer and the next three most highly compensated executive officers (excluding the principal financial officer).
Performance-based compensation in excess of $1,000,000 is deductible if specified criteria are met, including shareowner approval of applicable plans. In this regard, the PPL Corporation Short-term Incentive Plan is designed to enable us to make
cash awards to officers that are deductible under Section&nbsp;162(m). Similarly, the PPL Corporation Incentive Compensation Plan and the 2012&nbsp;Stock Incentive Plan are structured to enable grants of equity-based incentive awards to be
deductible under Section&nbsp;162(m). The Committee generally seeks ways to limit the impact of Section&nbsp;162(m). However, the Committee believes that the tax deduction limitation should not compromise our ability to establish and implement
incentive programs that support the compensation objectives discussed above. Accordingly, achieving these objectives and maintaining required flexibility in this regard may result in payments of compensation or grants of awards that are not
deductible for federal income tax purposes. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><I>Sections&nbsp;280G and 4999</I>. Several years ago, we entered into change in control agreements with
Messrs.&nbsp;Spence, Farr, Grey and DeCampli that provide benefits to the executives upon specified terminations of employment in connection with a change in control of PPL Corporation. The agreements provide for tax protection in the form of a
gross-up payment to reimburse the executive for any excise tax under Section&nbsp;4999 of the Internal Revenue Code, as well as any additional income and employment taxes resulting from such reimbursement. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Section&nbsp;4999 imposes a 20% non-deductible excise tax on the recipient of an &#147;excess parachute payment,&#148; and Section&nbsp;280G disallows the tax deduction
to the payor of any amount that is considered an excess parachute payment. Payments as a result of a change in control must equal or exceed three times the executive&#146;s base amount, a five-year average of the executive officer&#146;s
compensation as defined by the IRS, in order to be considered excess parachute payments, and then the lost deduction and excise tax is imposed on the parachute payments that exceed the executive&#146;s base amount. The intent of the tax gross-up
under the separation protection agreements is to make the executive officers whole for a tax penalty they may incur when receiving their contractual separation payments in the event of their displacement in connection with a change in control. We
believe the provision of tax protection for the adverse tax consequences imposed on the executive under these rules was consistent with market practice, has been an important executive retention component of our program and remains consistent with
our compensation objectives. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">For executives hired in 2012 or later, however, PPL will not provide executives with a gross-up payment in connection with
Section&nbsp;4999. The company entered into a new change in control agreement with Mr.&nbsp;Staffieri in 2013 in which there is no provision for gross-up payments. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">50&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;PPL Corporation 2014
Proxy Statement </P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><I>ASC Topic 718. </I>Under the guidance of Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) Accounting Standards Codification
(ASC) Topic 718, Compensation &#151; Stock Compensation (formerly, FASB Statement of Financial Accounting Standards No.&nbsp;123 (revised 2004), &#147;Share-Based Payment,&#148; which was known as SFAS&nbsp;123(R)), the following methods are used by
PPL to determine the aggregate grant date fair value of PPL&#146;s stock-based awards: (1)&nbsp;the closing market price of PPL common stock at the date of grant is used to value its restricted stock and restricted stock unit awards; (2)&nbsp;a
Monte Carlo pricing model that considers both implied and historical volatility over three years using daily stock price observations for PPL and all companies that are in the Philadelphia Stock Exchange Utility Index is used to determine the fair
value of each of its performance unit awards made through 2013; and (3)&nbsp;the Black-Scholes stock option pricing model is used to determine the fair value of its stock option awards. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL" ALIGN="right">PPL Corporation 2014 Proxy
Statement &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;51 </P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">

&#147;CD&amp;A&nbsp;&#151; 2013 Total Direct Compensation&nbsp;&#151; Long-Term Incentive Awards (Equity Awards) &#151; Performance Units&#148; beginning on page&nbsp;41. The payout range for performance unit awards granted in 2013 is from 25% to
200% of target. The performance period is 2013-2015. At the end of the performance period, PPL TSR for the three-year period is compared to the total return of the companies in the Philadelphia Stock Exchange Utility Index. Shares of PPL common
stock reflecting the applicable number of performance units, as well as reinvested cash or stock dividend equivalents, will vest and be paid according to the applicable level of achievement of the performance goal. If actual performance falls below
the 25% payout level, the payout would be zero. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

additional information, see &#147;CD&amp;A&nbsp;&#151; 2013 Total Direct Compensation&nbsp;&#151; Long-Term Incentive Awards (Equity Awards) &#151; Performance-Contingent Restricted Stock Units&#148; beginning on page&nbsp;40. In general,
restrictions on the awards will lapse on January&nbsp;24, 2016, three years from the date of grant. Each restricted stock unit entitles the executive to receive additional restricted stock units equal in value to the amount of quarterly dividends
paid on PPL common stock. These additional restricted stock units are payable in shares of PPL common stock at the end of the restriction period, subject to the same conditions as the underlying restricted stock units. </TD></TR></TABLE>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL" ALIGN="center"><B><A NAME="toc651955_60"></A>OUTSTANDING EQUITY AWARDS AT FISCAL YEAR-END 2013 </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The following table provides information on all unexercised stock option awards, as well as all unvested restricted stock and restricted stock unit awards and unearned
and unvested performance units, for each named executive officer as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. Each stock option grant, as well as each grant of performance units that is unearned and unvested, is shown separately for each named executive officer,
and the restricted stock or restricted stock units that have not vested are shown in the aggregate. The vesting schedule for each grant is shown following this table, based on the stock option, restricted stock or restricted stock unit award, or
performance unit award grant date. The market value of the stock awards is based on the closing price of PPL common stock on the NYSE as of December&nbsp;31, 2013, the last trading day of 2013, which was $30.09. For additional information about the
stock option and stock awards, see &#147;CD&amp;A&nbsp;&#151; 2013 Total Direct <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Compensation&nbsp;&#151;Long-Term</FONT> Incentive Awards (Equity Awards)&#148; beginning on page&nbsp;39. </P>

<TD WIDTH="4%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><SUP STYLE="font-size:85%; vertical-align:top">(3)</SUP>&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="left" STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt">All restricted stock units for the named executive officers under PPL&#146;s Incentive Compensation Plan and the 2012 Stock Incentive Plan vest on the third
anniversary of the grant date. The dates </P></TD></TR></TABLE>

Proxy Statement </P>

that restrictions lapse for each restricted stock or restricted stock unit award granted to the named executive officers are: </TD></TR></TABLE>

<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="4%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><SUP STYLE="font-size:85%; vertical-align:top">(4)</SUP>&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">The number of performance units granted in 2012 disclosed in the table for each named executive officer represents the target amount for 2012 awards. The target amount is used because PPL&#146;s total relative
shareowner return was below the 100th percentile, but above the threshold level, as compared to its industry peers for the time period of 2012 through 2013, the second year of the three-year performance period for the 2012 awards. The number of
performance units granted in 2013 disclosed in the table for each named executive officer represents the target amount for 2013 awards. The target amount is used because PPL&#146;s total relative shareowner return was below the 100th percentile, but
above the threshold level, as compared to its industry peers for 2013, the first year of the three-year performance period for the 2013 awards. Disclosure rules require amounts reported in the table for each year to be based on the threshold
attainment amount, except if performance exceeds the threshold amount but is below the target amount, the disclosure is based on the target attainment amount and if performance exceeds the target amount, the disclosure is based on the maximum
attainment amount. The number of shares shown in the table for each named executive officer also includes dividends reflected as additional performance units. </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">These performance units are payable in shares of PPL common stock following the performance period. While the performance period ends on
December&nbsp;31, 2014 for the 2012 awards and December&nbsp;31, 2015 for the 2013 awards, the number of performance units earned is not determined until the Compensation, Governance and Nominating Committee, or CGNC, certifies that the level of
performance goals have been achieved. The number of performance units earned at the time of certification may be more or less than the number of awards reflected in this table, depending on whether or not the performance goals have been achieved and
the level of achievement. See pages 41-43 of the CD&amp;A for a discussion of the performance goals related to TSR and the attainment levels for each award. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL" ALIGN="right">PPL Corporation 2014 Proxy
Statement &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;59 </P>

withholding tax and broker commissions. </P> <P STYLE="font-size:12pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

<P STYLE="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL" ALIGN="center"><B><A NAME="toc651955_62"></A>PENSION BENEFITS IN 2013 </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The
following table sets forth information on the pension benefits for the named executive officers under each of the following pension plans: </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="5%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"><B>PPL Retirement Plan. </B>The PPL Retirement Plan is a funded and tax-qualified defined benefit retirement plan that covers approximately 5,574 active employees as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. The PPL Retirement Plan
was closed to new salaried employees after December&nbsp;31, 2011. As applicable to Messrs.&nbsp;Spence, Farr, Grey and DeCampli, the plan provides benefits based primarily on a formula that takes into account the executive&#146;s earnings for each
fiscal year. Benefits under the PPL Retirement Plan for eligible employees are determined as the greater of the following two formulas: </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="14%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">The first is a &#147;career average pay formula&#148; of 2.25% of annual earnings for each year of credited service under the plan. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="14%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">The second is a &#147;final average pay formula&#148; as follows: </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:17%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">1.3%&nbsp;of final average earnings
up to the Average Social Security Wage Base </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:17%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>plus </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:17%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">1.7%&nbsp;of final average earnings in excess of the Average Social Security Wage Base </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:17%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>multiplied by </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:17%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">the sum of years of credited
service (up to a maximum of 40&nbsp;years). </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:14%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Under the final average pay formula, &#147;final average earnings&#148; equal the average of the
highest 60&nbsp;months of pay during the last 120&nbsp;months of credited service. The Average </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">60&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;PPL Corporation 2014
Proxy Statement </P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:14%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">
Social Security Wage Base is the average of the taxable Social Security Wage Base for the 35&nbsp;consecutive years preceding an employee&#146;s retirement date or, for employees retiring at the
end of 2013, $67,308. The executive&#146;s annual earnings taken into account under each formula include base salary and cash incentive awards, but may not exceed an IRS-prescribed limit applicable to tax-qualified plans ($255,000 for 2013). </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The benefit an employee earns is payable starting at retirement on a monthly basis for life. Benefits are computed on the basis of the life annuity form
of pension, with a normal retirement age of 65. Benefits are reduced for retirement prior to age&nbsp;60 for employees with 20&nbsp;years of credited service and reduced prior to age&nbsp;65 for other employees. Employees vest in the PPL Retirement
Plan after five years of credited service. In addition, the plan provides for joint and survivor annuity choices and does not require employee contributions. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Benefits under the PPL Retirement Plan are subject to the limitations imposed under Section&nbsp;415 of the Internal Revenue Code. The Section&nbsp;415
limit for 2013 was $205,000 per year for a single life annuity payable at an IRS-prescribed retirement age. Benefits in excess of these federal limits are payable from company funds under the Supplemental Compensation Pension Plan described below
unless the employee is eligible for benefits under the PPL Supplemental Executive Retirement Plan described below. </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="5%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"><B>Supplemental Compensation Pension Plan. </B>The PPL Supplemental Compensation Pension Plan is a non-qualified plan that applies to approximately 63 active employees hired prior to January&nbsp;1, 2012 who are vested
in the PPL Retirement Plan and, in the case of Mr.&nbsp;Farr, is not vested in the PPL Supplemental Executive Retirement Plan, or SERP, at the time of termination or retirement. The benefit formula is the same as the PPL Retirement Plan, but it
reflects compensation in excess of the IRS-prescribed limit of $255,000 for 2013. The plan benefit is calculated using all PPL affiliated company service, not just service credited under the PPL Retirement Plan. Upon retirement, this plan will only
pay out the &#147;excess&#148; benefit above and beyond the PPL Retirement Plan. At such time as Mr.&nbsp;Farr vests in the SERP, he will no longer be eligible for this plan. </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="5%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"><B>PPL Supplemental Executive Retirement Plan. </B>The company offered the SERP to approximately 20 active officers as of December&nbsp;31, 2013, including Messrs.&nbsp;Spence, Farr, Grey and DeCampli, to provide for
retirement benefits above amounts available under the PPL Retirement Plan described above. The SERP is unfunded and is not qualified for tax purposes. Accrued benefits under the SERP are subject to claims of the company&#146;s creditors in the event
of bankruptcy. The SERP was closed to new officers after December&nbsp;31, 2011. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The SERP formula is 2.0% of final average earnings
for the first 20&nbsp;years of credited service plus 1.5% of final average earnings for the next 10&nbsp;years. &#147;Final average earnings&#148; is the average of the highest 60&nbsp;months of earnings during the last 120&nbsp;months of credited
service. &#147;Earnings&#148; include base salary and annual cash incentive awards. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Benefits are computed on the basis of the life annuity form of
pension, with a normal retirement age of 65. Generally, no benefit is payable under the SERP if the executive officer has less than 10&nbsp;years of service unless specifically authorized, such as upon a qualifying termination in connection with a
change in control. Benefits under the SERP are paid, in accordance with a participant&#146;s advance election, as a single sum or as an annuity, including choices of a joint and survivor or years-certain annuity. At age&nbsp;60, or at age&nbsp;50
with 10&nbsp;years of service, accrued benefits are vested and may not be reduced by an amendment to the SERP or termination by the company. After the completion of 10&nbsp;years of service, participants are eligible for death benefit protection.
</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The company does not have a policy for granting additional years of service but has done so under the SERP in individual situations. A grant of
additional years of service to any executive </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL" ALIGN="right">PPL Corporation 2014 Proxy
Statement &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;61 </P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">
officer must be approved by the Compensation, Governance and Nominating Committee, or the CGNC. The CGNC granted Mr.&nbsp;Spence an additional year of service for each year of employment under
the SERP as a retention mechanism. Mr.&nbsp;DeCampli was granted additional service for purposes of determining his eligibility for a SERP retirement benefit equal to four years of additional service upon reaching age 55, and additional service for
determining his benefit upon retirement equal to five years of additional service upon reaching age 56. The total SERP benefit cannot increase beyond 30&nbsp;years of service for any participant. The table below reflects the additional service
amounts based on service as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. Please refer to the table footnotes for additional information related to credited service under the SERP. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Mr.&nbsp;Grey is credited with service under the SERP commencing as of age&nbsp;30, based on plan provisions in effect prior to January&nbsp;1, 1998.
</P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="5%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"><B>PPL Subsidiary Retirement Plan. </B>The PPL Subsidiary Retirement Plan, in which Mr.&nbsp;Farr became a participant before he became an officer of the company, is a defined benefit plan that covers approximately 467
active employees as of December&nbsp;31, 2013 and utilizes a hypothetical account balance to determine a monthly retirement annuity when an individual retires (known as a &#147;cash balance plan&#148;). The PPL Subsidiary Retirement Plan was closed
to new salaried employees after December&nbsp;31, 2011. Age&nbsp;65 is the normal retirement age, but an individual may receive a reduced benefit as early as age&nbsp;50 if the participant has at least five years of service. </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The benefit formula for yearly increases to the hypothetical account balance is an increasing scale, based on age plus years of service. A participant
whose age plus years of service is 32&nbsp;or lower receives the minimum yearly credit of 5% of compensation plus 1.5% of compensation that is in excess of 50% of the Social Security Wage Base for that year. &#147;Compensation&#148; generally means
base salary. The amount credited increases as age plus years of service increases, up to a maximum credit, at age plus years of service of 75 or above, of 14% of compensation plus 6% of compensation that is in excess of 50% of the Social Security
Wage Base. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">A participant has a vested right to a benefit under this plan after three years of service. Benefits are paid as a monthly annuity amount
for life, or as a joint and survivor annuity. The amount of the annuity is determined by converting the hypothetical account balance, plus an assumed rate of interest, into a monthly annuity for life or joint lives at the participant&#146;s date of
commencement of payment. </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="5%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"><B>LG&amp;E and KU Retirement Plan</B>. The LG&amp;E and KU Retirement Plan, or LG&amp;E Retirement Plan, is a funded and tax-qualified defined benefit retirement plan that covers approximately 1,770 active employees as
of December&nbsp;31, 2013 and that was closed to new participants on December&nbsp;31, 2005. As applicable to Mr.&nbsp;Staffieri, the LG&amp;E Retirement Plan provides benefits based on a formula that takes into account the executive&#146;s average
monthly earnings and years of service. Benefits for eligible employees are determined as the greater of the following two formulas: </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="14%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">The first formula is 1.58% of average monthly earnings plus 0.40% of average monthly earnings in excess of &#147;covered compensation&#148; multiplied by years of credited service (up to a maximum of 30&nbsp;years).
</TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="14%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">The second formula is 1.68% of average monthly earnings multiplied by years of credited service (up to a maximum of 30&nbsp;years). </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Under the LG&amp;E Retirement Plan, the &#147;average monthly earnings&#148; is the average of the highest five consecutive monthly earnings prior to
termination of employment. &#147;Monthly earnings&#148; is defined as total compensation as indicated on Form&nbsp;W-2 including deferrals to a </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">62&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;PPL Corporation 2014
Proxy Statement </P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">
401(k) plan, but excluding any earnings from the exercise of stock options, limited to the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">IRS-prescribed</FONT> limit applicable to tax-qualified plans ($255,000
for 2013), divided by 12. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">&#147;Covered compensation&#148; is one-twelfth of the average of the Social Security Wage Base for the 35-year period
ending with the year of a participant&#146;s social security retirement age. The Social Security Wage Base for future years is assumed to be equal to the Social Security Wage Base of the current year. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The benefit an employee earns is payable starting at retirement on a monthly basis for life. Benefits are calculated on the basis of the life annuity
form of pension with a normal retirement age of 65. Early retirement occurs at the earlier of age&nbsp;55 or 30&nbsp;years of service. There is no early retirement reduction after attainment of age&nbsp;62. Prior to age 62, benefits are reduced.
Employees vest in the LG&amp;E Retirement Plan after five years of service. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Benefits under the LG&amp;E Retirement Plan are subject to the
limitations imposed under Section&nbsp;415 of the Internal Revenue Code. The Section&nbsp;415 limit for 2013 is $205,000 per year for a single life annuity payable at an IRS-prescribed retirement age. </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="5%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"><B>LG&amp;E and KU Supplemental Executive Retirement Plan</B>. Mr.&nbsp;Staffieri is a participant in the LG&amp;E and KU Supplemental Executive Retirement Plan, or LG&amp;E SERP. The LG&amp;E SERP is unfunded and is
not qualified for tax purposes. Accrued benefits under the LG&amp;E SERP are subject to claims of the company&#146;s creditors in the event of bankruptcy. </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The LG&amp;E SERP formula is equal to 64% of the average monthly compensation less </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="13%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">100% of the monthly qualified LG&amp;E Retirement Plan benefit payable at age&nbsp;65; </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="13%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">100% of the primary Social Security Benefit payable at age&nbsp;65; </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="13%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">100% of any matching contribution or the employer contribution for those participants for whom the defined contribution plan is the primary retirement vehicle;&nbsp;and </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:10pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="13%">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#149;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">100% of any other employer-provided benefit payable at age&nbsp;65 as a life annuity from any qualified defined benefit plan or defined contribution plan (provided such qualified defined contribution plan was the
employer&#146;s primary vehicle for retirement) sponsored by previous employers. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The net benefit is multiplied by a fraction, not to
exceed one, the numerator of which is years of service at date of termination and the denominator is 15. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">&#147;Average monthly compensation&#148; is
the average compensation for the highest 36 consecutive months preceding termination of employment. &#147;Compensation&#148; is defined as base salary plus short-term incentive pay prior to any deferrals under any qualified or non-qualified deferred
compensation plan. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL" ALIGN="right">PPL Corporation 2014 Proxy
Statement &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;63 </P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:8%; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Normal retirement is age&nbsp;65. Early retirement for a participant who has been credited with at least
five years of service and whose age is at least age&nbsp;50 is the later of separation of service or age&nbsp;55. There is no early retirement reduction after attainment of age&nbsp;62. </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:12pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

lump-sum payment, but a lump-sum amount is shown here for comparison purposes. See note (c)&nbsp;below for Mr.&nbsp;Staffieri&#146;s monthly LG&amp;E SERP benefits. The&nbsp;amounts shown in this table represent the values that would have become
payable based on a December&nbsp;31, 2013 termination of employment. Actual payment would be made following December&nbsp;31, subject to plan rules and in compliance with Section&nbsp;409A of the Internal Revenue Code. </TD></TR></TABLE>

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">Messrs.&nbsp;Spence, Farr and DeCampli are not eligible to retire under the PPL SERP. Messrs.&nbsp;Spence and DeCampli, however, are vested under the SERP, while Mr.&nbsp;Farr is not. If Mr.&nbsp;Spence had left the
company on December&nbsp;31, 2013, voluntarily or as a result of a disability or death, he, or his spouse, would have been vested in a deferred benefit under the PPL Retirement Plan and the SERP. If Mr.&nbsp;Farr had left the company voluntarily on
December&nbsp;31, 2013, he would have been eligible for benefits under the PPL Retirement Plan, the PPL Subsidiary Retirement Plan and the PPL Supplemental Compensation Pension Plan, but not under the PPL SERP. Additionally, if Mr.&nbsp;Farr had
died on December&nbsp;31, 2013, his spouse would have been eligible for a PPL SERP benefit in addition to a benefit from the PPL Retirement Plan and PPL Subsidiary Retirement Plan. If Mr.&nbsp;DeCampli had left the company on December&nbsp;31, 2013,
voluntarily or as a result of a disability or death, he, or his spouse, would have been vested in a deferred benefit under the PPL Retirement Plan and the SERP. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

on December&nbsp;31, 2013, the monthly LG&amp;E SERP disability benefit payable at age&nbsp;65 as a life annuity (assuming continued accrual) would have been $82,948. </TD></TR></TABLE>

<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL" ALIGN="center"><B><A NAME="toc651955_63"></A>NONQUALIFIED DEFERRED COMPENSATION IN 2013 </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">The PPL Executive Deferred Compensation Plan allows participants to defer all or a portion of their cash compensation in excess of the required minimum payroll taxes.
In addition, the company made matching contributions to this plan during 2013 of up to 3% of an executive&#146;s cash compensation (base salary plus annual cash incentive award) to match executive contributions that would have been made to
PPL&#146;s tax-qualified 401(k) deferred savings plan, also known as the Deferred Savings Plan, except for Internal Revenue Service-imposed limitations on those contributions. The PPL Executive Deferred Compensation Plan is unfunded and is not
qualified for tax purposes. All benefits under this plan are subject to the claims of the company&#146;s creditors in the event of bankruptcy. A hypothetical account is established for each participant who elects to defer, and the participant
selects one or more deemed investment choices that generally mirror those that are available to employees under the Deferred Savings Plan at Fidelity Investments. These investment accounts include large, mid and small cap investment funds,
international equity index funds, target date funds, bond funds and a stable value fund, with returns that ranged from -2.18% to 37.83% during 2013. Earnings and losses on each account are determined based on the performance of the investment funds
selected by the participant. The company maintains each account as a bookkeeping entry. During 2013, Messrs.&nbsp;Spence, Farr, Grey and DeCampli notionally invested in one or more of those funds. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">In general, the named executive officers who participate in this plan cannot withdraw any amounts from their deferred accounts until they either leave or retire from
the company. The company&#146;s Corporate Leadership Council, which currently consists of the chief executive officer, chief financial officer and general counsel, has the discretion to make a &#147;hardship distribution&#148; if there is an
unforeseeable emergency that causes a severe financial hardship to the participant. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Participants may elect distribution in one or more annual installments for a
period of up to 15&nbsp;years, provided the participant complies with the election and timing rules of Section&nbsp;409A of the Internal Revenue Code. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Mr.&nbsp;Staffieri is a participant in the LG&amp;E and KU Nonqualified Savings Plan. The plan allows participants to defer up to a maximum of 75% of base salary and
annual cash incentive awards. In addition, the participant receives a matching contribution equal to 70% of the first 6% deferred if that the participant is not eligible for matching contributions in the LG&amp;E and KU Savings Plan (a tax-qualified
401(k) plan) at the time the deferred compensation would have otherwise been paid to the participant. The LG&amp;E and KU Nonqualified Savings Plan is unfunded and is not qualified for tax purposes. All benefits under the LG&amp;E and KU
Nonqualified Savings Plan are subject to the claims of creditors in the event of bankruptcy. A hypothetical account is established for each participant who elects to defer. The amount in the participant&#146;s hypothetical account is credited with
interest at an annual rate equal to the Prime Interest Rate as reported in <I>The Wall Street Journal</I>. The Prime Interest Rate will be reset quarterly based on the last day of the preceding calendar quarter or
March&nbsp;31,&nbsp;June&nbsp;30,&nbsp;September&nbsp;30, and December&nbsp;31. The interest shall be calculated by applying the Prime Interest Rate to the balance in the hypothetical account. Mr.&nbsp;Staffieri&#146;s rate of return for 2013 was
3.25%. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">In general, distributions under the LG&amp;E and KU Nonqualified Savings Plan are made as specified by the named executive officer at the time of completion
of the deferral agreement. However, a &#147;hardship distribution&#148; will be approved by the Employee Benefits Plan Board if there is an unforeseeable emergency, as defined by Section&nbsp;409A of the Internal Revenue Code, that causes a severe
financial hardship to the participant. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">66&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;PPL Corporation 2014
Proxy Statement </P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL">Participants may elect a lump-sum payment or annual installment payments for a period of not less than two years and not
more than 10&nbsp;years, provided the participant complies with the election and timing rules of Section&nbsp;409A of the Internal Revenue Code. </P> <P STYLE="font-size:12pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
